chronology.reason_created: CRE-002 exposed five commitments not explicitly licensed by Baseline 1.0
chronology.first_eligible_experiment: CRE-002-EXT-001 after independent preregistration, checksum lock, review, and execution unlock
chronology.cannot_reclassify: any previously recorded CRE-002 candidate outcome

scope.claim_subject: official vocabulary primitives plus the explicitly declared derived constructs in this baseline

derived_constructs.formal_definition: A finite, explicitly enumerated set of alternative outcomes attached to one transition point. Exactly one listed alternative is selected per firing; no probability distribution, hidden choice rule, fairness rule, or unlisted outcome is licensed.
operational_constraints: selection creates no extra state beyond the selected recorded outcome

derived_constructs.formal_definition: A bounded interleaving semantics over a finite set of atomic transitions. Concurrent eligibility permits multiple next transitions, but each execution step applies exactly one complete atomic transition; simultaneous writes and continuous time are not licensed.

A finite defeasible ordering among explicitly identified applicable rules. A higher-priority applicable rule may defeat a lower-priority conflicting rule only for the current state and only when the registered conflict predicate holds.

A finite evidence record binding a claim value to an explicit source identifier and reliability label. Guards may inspect the exact recorded tuple; no source credibility, truth, or causal authority beyond the registered label is inferred.
operational_constraints: updates append rather than rewrite prior evidence

derived_constructs.formal_definition: A bounded transition whose explicit effect changes the active status or registered parameter of an identified rule. The permitted modification targets, operation count, before-state requirements, and later admissibility effects are all finite and explicit.
operational_constraints: later rule eligibility reads the modified status

CRE-001-VOCAB-A-1.0.construction_rule: Objects identify states, rules, evidence and alternatives; Relations encode finite guards, provenance, priority, conflicts and ordering; Transformations encode atomic transitions and bounded rule-status updates.
CRE-001-VOCAB-A-1.0.limitation: Licensing applies only when every required field and operational constraint of the selected derived construct is explicitly represented.

CRE-001-VOCAB-B-1.0.construction_rule: States hold bounded assignments; Transitions encode atomic moves and rule-status updates; Labels encode finite alternatives, provenance tuples, priority/conflict guards and ordered-history markers.
CRE-001-VOCAB-B-1.0.limitation: Labels may not function as unrestricted payload containers; each label must use a registered finite schema.

CRE-001-VOCAB-C-1.0.construction_rule: Representations identify values, rules and evidence; Representational Structures record finite organization and order; Interpretations assign registered meanings; the Investigation fixes scope; Calculus records atomic eligibility, updates, defeasible priority and bounded modification.
CRE-001-VOCAB-C-1.0.limitation: Calculus is restricted to the explicitly registered finite operations and cannot import a general-purpose programming language.

freeze_requirements: all five constructs have positive examples and non-examples
all three vocabulary licensing records are complete
CRE-002 original result remains byte-for-byte unchanged
